content.title:
Run $tests = @(
  @('fred','--group','rates')
  @('fred','--group','housing')
  @('fred','--group','labor')
  @('alphavantage','--quote','SPY')
  @('treasury','--yields')
  @('openfema','--recent')
  @('usaspending','--recent')
  @('fda','--recent-approvals')
  @('sec','--thesis')
  @('clinicaltrials','--oncology')
  @('clinicaltrials','--amr')
  @('uspto','--ptab')
  @('arxiv','--drones')
  @('arxiv','--amr')
  @('pubmed','--amr')
  @('pubmed','--psychedelics')
  @('newsapi','--topic','business')
)
$failed = @()
foreach ($t in $tests) {
  $label = $t -join ' '
  Write-Host "`n>>> node run.mjs $label"
  & node run.mjs @t
  $code = $LASTEXITCODE
  Write-Host "<<< exit $code"
  if ($code -ne 0) {
    $failed += $label
  }
}
Write-Host "`nMorning sequence failures: $($failed.Count)"
foreach ($f in $failed) {
  Write-Host "FAIL $f"
}
if ($failed.Count -gt 0) {
  exit 1
}

content.text:
**Findings**
- High: `sam --all` can fail both upstream calls and still exit `0`, so automation can go false-green. The entity and opportunities sub-pullers catch `HTTP 404` and return empty success objects instead of rethrowing, and the top-level command treats that as success. See [sam.mjs#L30](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L30), [sam.mjs#L121](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L121), [sam.mjs#L239](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L239). In the smoke test, `node run.mjs sam --all` printed two `HTTP 404` failures and still returned success.
- High: `fmp --options` masks a hard provider failure as success, while other FMP paths fail correctly. The options path catches provider errors and returns `{ filePath: null, signals: [] }`, so the CLI exits `0` even on a hard `403`. See [fmp.mjs#L186](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L186) and [fmp.mjs#L193](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L193). In testing, `node run.mjs fmp --options SPY` exited cleanly after an FMP legacy-endpoint `403`, while `node run.mjs fmp --profile AAPL` failed as expected.
- Medium: Several wrapper pullers drop their structured return values, which breaks orchestration and provenance tracking. One concrete impact is the housing playbook: the FEMA step succeeds, but the synthesized report loses the generated filename and records only `OK`. See [openfema.mjs#L14](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\openfema.mjs#L14), [sec.mjs#L454](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sec.mjs#L454), [Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L17](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\05_Data_Pulls\Housing\2026-03-27_Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L17), [Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L58](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\05_Data_Pulls\Housing\2026-03-27_Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L58).
- Medium: `sam --entities` is a latent schema violation. If that endpoint starts returning data again, the generated note will be missing required pull-note fields `signal_status` and `signals`. See [sam.mjs#L93](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L93) and [schema.mjs#L49](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\lib\schema.mjs#L49).
- Medium: There is no real automated test entrypoint for the pull system. The repo only defines a `pull` script, and the validator only checks existing markdown, not generator behavior or live endpoint health. See [package.json#L5](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\package.json#L5), [validate-vault.mjs#L21](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\validate-vault.mjs#L21). I had to build a manual smoke pass to get end-to-end coverage.

**Tests**
- `node run.mjs status`: passed, reported `20/22` sources ready.
- `node run.mjs validate`: passed before and after the smoke run.
- Documented 17-command morning sequence: all commands completed successfully.
- Additional smoke pass:
  `bea --gdp`, `eia --all`, `cboe --all`, `socrata --permits`, and `playbook housing-cycle` succeeded.
  `fmp --profile AAPL` failed with provider `403`.
  `sam --all` and `fmp --options SPY` returned success despite real upstream failures.

**Notes**
- The smoke run generated new dated notes under `05_Data_Pulls/` and `06_Signals/`. No code was changed.
- There were pre-existing Obsidian plugin changes under `.obsidian/plugins/agent-client/`; I left those untouched.

If you want, I can patch the masked-failure paths and the missing return contracts next, then rerun the same smoke pass.
Yeah fix the remaining failures

I’m fixing the actual failure modes now: masked success on `sam` and `fmp`, plus the dropped return values that break orchestration. After the edits I’ll rerun the same smoke paths to confirm behavior changed for the right reasons.

content.text:
Fixed the code-controlled failures. Wrapper pullers now return structured results instead of dropping them, so orchestration keeps file provenance and signals. The key fixes are in [openfema.mjs#L16](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\openfema.mjs#L16), [usaspending.mjs#L16](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\usaspending.mjs#L16), and [sec.mjs#L492](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sec.mjs#L492). The housing playbook now carries the FEMA pull through correctly; the regenerated report includes `FEMA_Declarations.md` in `related_pulls` and the pull-status table at [2026-03-27_Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L21](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\05_Data_Pulls\Housing\2026-03-27_Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L21) and [2026-03-27_Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L73](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\05_Data_Pulls\Housing\2026-03-27_Housing_Cycle_Report.md#L73).

I also fixed the false-green behavior. FMP profile/income now use the current stable base URL in [fmp.mjs#L39](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L39), [fmp.mjs#L42](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L42), [fmp.mjs#L101](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L101), and [fmp.mjs#L104](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L104), which made `node run.mjs fmp --profile AAPL` pass. The options path and SAM path now fail explicitly instead of pretending success, at [fmp.mjs#L191](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\fmp.mjs#L191), [sam.mjs#L125](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L125), and [sam.mjs#L251](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L251). I also brought the SAM request shapes closer to the public docs in [sam.mjs#L74](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L74) and [sam.mjs#L154](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L154), and fixed the latent schema issue for entity notes in [sam.mjs#L101](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts\pullers\sam.mjs#L101).

Retest status: `validate`, `fmp --profile AAPL`, `playbook housing-cycle`, `bea --gdp`, `treasury --yields`, and `usaspending --recent` pass. Remaining failures are upstream/provider-side: `fmp --options SPY` still returns FMP’s legacy-endpoint `403`, and `sam --all` / `sam --opportunities` still return `404` from SAM despite the URL updates. Those are no longer masked, but they are not fixable locally without a working provider endpoint/account path.

Okay next task is to clean up to make everything token efficent. Review the plan outlined below and create a implementation plan:

Absolutely. Here’s an agent-neutral version that swaps out Claude-specific language for any AI agent, assistant, or automation system.
 
 
AI Agent Guide: Implement a Token-Efficient Obsidian Vault
 
 
 
Objective
 
 
Restructure an Obsidian vault so it is easy for AI agents to navigate with minimal context waste.
 
 
Design principles
 
 
Use short, predictable filenames with prefixes:

Use hyphens only. Avoid spaces and underscores in normal filenames. Keep filenames to about 4 to 5 words where possible.
Use shallow, numbered folders:

Keep depth to a maximum of 2 folder levels where possible.
Organize folders by note type, not by topic. Topics should be grouped through MOC files, not topic folders.
Put the most important control files at the top:

_index.md should be the main entry point. Detailed behavior instructions should live in a separate file so the index stays lightweight.
Exclude expensive or low-value areas from normal agent scanning:

 
 
 
 
Target vault structure
 
/
├── _index.md
├── AGENT.md
├── _agent-instructions.md
├── 000-moc/
│   ├── moc-dashboard.md
│   ├── moc-ai-tools.md
│   ├── moc-projects.md
│   └── moc-areas.md
├── 100-admin/
│   ├── ref-guidelines.md
│   └── ref-system-rules.md
├── 200-projects/
│   ├── prj-website-rebrand.md
│   ├── prj-client-onboarding.md
│   └── prj-content-engine.md
├── 300-references/
│   ├── ref-obsidian.md
│   ├── ref-ai-automation.md
│   └── ref-workflows.md
├── 400-templates/
│   ├── tpl-project.md
│   ├── tpl-reference.md
│   └── tpl-moc.md
├── 500-archive/
│   └── arc-old-project-notes.md
└── 600-attachments/
    ├── image-001.png
    └── doc-brief.pdf
 
 
 
What the agent should do
 
 
 
Phase 1: Audit the vault
 
 
Scan all notes and folders.
Build an inventory with:
Detect problems:

 
 
 
Phase 2: Create the control files
 
 
Create these files if they do not exist.
 
 
_index.md
 
Keep it brief. It should point to the main MOCs and state exclusions.
 
Example:
# Vault Index
Start here.
Core maps:
- [[000-moc/moc-dashboard]]
- [[000-moc/moc-projects]]
- [[000-moc/moc-ai-tools]]
- [[000-moc/moc-areas]]
Rules:
- Do not load files from `/500-archive/` or `/600-attachments/` unless explicitly requested.
- Read this file first, then open only the MOC files relevant to the task.
 
AGENT.md
 
Use this as the general operating note for any AI system.
 
Example:
# AGENT.md
Vault operating rules:
- Read `_index.md` first.
- Load only the minimum set of notes needed for the task.
- Prefer `000-moc/` notes before opening detailed notes.
- Do not load `/500-archive/` or `/600-attachments/` unless explicitly asked.
- Prefer concise, structured outputs unless a different format is requested.
 
_agent-instructions.md
 
Put detailed behavior, formatting preferences, workflow rules, and output style guidance here instead of in _index.md.
 
 
 
Naming rules for the agent
 
 
Apply these renaming rules:
Rule A: Prefix by type
- MOC        -> moc-
- Project    -> prj-
- Reference  -> ref-
- Template   -> tpl-
- Archive    -> arc-
Rule B: Kebab-case only
- lowercase
- hyphens only
- no spaces
- no underscores unless using a leading "_" for priority files
Rule C: Keep names short
- target 2 to 5 meaningful terms after prefix
Rule D: Dates belong in frontmatter, not filenames
- exception: daily notes, journals, logs where date is the identifier
Rule E: Priority files may use a leading underscore
- _index.md
- _agent-instructions.md
 
 
 
Folder routing rules for the agent
 
 
Use this routing logic:
if file is a map of content:
    move to /000-moc/
elif file is admin/system guidance:
    move to /100-admin/
elif file is an active project or client work note:
    move to /200-projects/ or /200-clients/
elif file is evergreen knowledge/reference:
    move to /300-references/
elif file is a reusable template:
    move to /400-templates/
elif file is inactive/completed:
    move to /500-archive/
elif file is an image, pdf, media, or binary:
    move to /600-attachments/
Keep folder depth shallow. Avoid topic folders like /ai-tools/chatgpt/notes/. Topic grouping should happen in MOCs instead.
 
 
 
MOC creation rules
 
 
The biggest structural move is to replace scattered topic navigation with MOCs. One good MOC can save an agent from opening a flock of tiny notes 🐦
 
 
The agent should:
 
 
Identify major topic areas.
Create one MOC per major area in 000-moc/.
Write each MOC as a dense briefing, not a scrapbook of links.
Include:

 
 
 
Example MOC template
 
---
title: AI Tools
type: moc
tags: [ai-tools]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# AI Tools
Summary:
- Central reference for AI tools used in workflows and writing.
- Priority tools: assistant platforms, automations, and local knowledge systems.
- Current focus: reduce context cost and improve task-specific prompting.
Key notes:
- [[ref-obsidian]]
- [[ref-ai-automation]]
- [[ref-workflows]]
Open questions:
- Which tools need dedicated project notes?
- Which tools should move to archive?
Decisions:
- Use MOC-first navigation.
- Keep instructions outside reference notes.
- Prefer brief summaries over raw source dumps.
 
 
 
Note formatting rules
 
 
The agent should rewrite or normalize notes to fit these rules:
 
Put the most important information in the first 3 to 5 lines.
Keep frontmatter lean:
Remove unnecessary frontmatter like mood, aliases, word-count, or decorative metadata.
Do not repeat the title in the opening paragraph.
Prefer bullets for list-like content.
Remove raw URLs, long quotes, and half-finished thoughts where they do not help complete tasks.
Split notes by use when needed:

 
 
 
Example normalized note
 
---
title: Obsidian
type: reference
tags: [knowledge-system]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# Obsidian
Summary:
- Local-first knowledge system used to store notes, references, and project materials.
- Works best for AI workflows when notes are short, structured, and linked through MOCs.
- Avoid broad folder nesting and unnecessary attachments in active areas.
Best uses:
- Personal knowledge management
- Project support
- Structured reference storage
Limits:
- Becomes harder for agents to navigate when folders reflect topics instead of note types.
- Large attachments and scattered notes increase context cost.
Related:
- [[moc-ai-tools]]
 
 
 
Tag rules
 
 
The agent should:
 
reduce tags to 3 to 5 maximum
normalize tags to kebab-case
merge duplicates like:

 
 
 
 
Attachment handling rules
 
 
The agent should:
 
Move all images, PDFs, media, and binary files into 600-attachments/.
Replace embedded images in active notes with short text descriptions where possible.
Add or preserve references to attachments without placing them in active knowledge folders.
 
 
 
 
Migration workflow the agent can follow
 
1. Create missing top-level folders.
2. Create `_index.md`, `AGENT.md`, `_agent-instructions.md` if absent.
3. Inventory all notes and attachments.
4. Classify each item by type.
5. Rename files to prefix + kebab-case format.
6. Move files into the correct numbered folder.
7. Move all binaries/media to `600-attachments/`.
8. Move inactive/completed material to `500-archive/`.
9. Generate MOCs for major topic areas.
10. Rewrite MOCs and key notes with dense summaries and lean frontmatter.
11. Normalize tags.
12. Add exclusion rules to `_index.md` and `AGENT.md`.
13. Produce a migration report with:
    - renamed files
    - moved files
    - created MOCs
    - archived items
    - unresolved conflicts
 
 
 
Prompt you can give an AI agent
 
You are restructuring an Obsidian vault for AI-efficient navigation.
Goal:
Implement a numbered, shallow vault structure with MOC-first navigation, lean metadata, and explicit exclusions for archive and attachments.
Required structure:
- _index.md
- AGENT.md
- _agent-instructions.md
- 000-moc/
- 100-admin/
- 200-projects/
- 300-references/
- 400-templates/
- 500-archive/
- 600-attachments/
Rules:
- Use short kebab-case filenames.
- Prefix note filenames by type: moc-, prj-, ref-, tpl-, arc-.
- Keep filenames short, usually 4 to 5 words max.
- Keep folder depth to max 2 levels where possible.
- Organize folders by note type, not by topic.
- Put topic organization into MOC files in 000-moc/.
- Keep frontmatter lean: title, type, tags, last_updated.
- Keep tags broad and limited to 3 to 5 per note.
- Move inactive notes to 500-archive/.
- Move all attachments and media to 600-attachments/.
- Add exclusions for /500-archive/ and /600-attachments/ in _index.md and AGENT.md.
- Rewrite key notes so the first 3 to 5 lines contain a dense summary.
- Prefer bullet points over padded prose.
- Do not duplicate titles in body text.
Deliverables:
1. Proposed file move and rename plan.
2. New folder structure.
3. Generated _index.md.
4. Generated AGENT.md.
5. Generated _agent-instructions.md.
6. Created MOC files for major topics.
7. Migration report with conflicts and assumptions.
If you want, I can also turn this into a cleaner copy-paste Markdown document with all Claude mentions fully scrubbed and the wording tightened for direct use in Obsidian.
AITools
AI_tools
artificialintelligencetools

content.text:
Context compacted
The plan is directionally right, but I would not apply it literally to this vault in one pass.

This vault is already shallow and numbered, and its graph logic is explicitly organized by domain in [Graph Conventions.md](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\04_Reference\Graph Conventions.md). That matters because `08_Entities`, `09_Macro`, `10_Theses`, templates, and Dataview-style linking appear to be part of the operating model described in [README.md](C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\README.md). A full “folder by note type” migration would create token-efficiency gains for agents, but it also risks breaking graph semantics, queries, templates, and pull/report workflows.

**Recommendation**
Adopt the token-efficient control layer first, then migrate only the parts of the vault that are safe to normalize. In practice: keep the current domain folders for operational and graph-critical content, and add an agent-first navigation surface on top.

**Implementation Plan**
1. Baseline audit and freeze rules.
   - Inventory all notes, generated outputs, templates, scripts, and non-note directories.
   - Classify each path as `control`, `reference`, `template`, `project`, `generated`, `graph-core`, or `archive`.
   - Define “do not move in phase 1”: `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, `scripts/`, `05_Data_Pulls/`, `06_Signals/`, and likely `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, `10_Theses/`.

2. Create the token-efficient control surface.
   - Add `_index.md`, `AGENT.md`, and `_agent-instructions.md` at the vault root.
   - Keep these files short and operational.
   - Use them to direct agents into a minimal subset of notes instead of scanning the vault broadly.

3. Add explicit scan exclusions.
   - Exclude `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, `scripts/`, `05_Data_Pulls/`, `06_Signals/`, `500-archive/` if created, and any future `600-attachments/`.
   - Treat generated notes as read-on-demand only, not default context.

4. Build MOC-first navigation over the current structure.
   - Create a `000-moc/` layer without immediately replacing the existing numbered folders.
   - Start with a small set: `moc-dashboard`, `moc-data-sources`, `moc-projects`, `moc-entities`, `moc-macro`, `moc-theses`, `moc-playbooks`.
   - Each MOC should summarize what the area contains, which notes matter first, and what should usually be ignored.

5. Normalize safe note classes first.
   - Target `00_Dashboard`, `03_Templates`, `04_Reference`, and selected `02_Projects` notes.
   - Apply lean frontmatter: `title`, `type`, `tags`, `last_updated`.
   - Normalize tags to kebab-case and reduce tag count.
   - Rewrite the first 3 to 5 lines of key notes into dense summaries.

6. Standardize filenames where risk is low.
   - Safe candidates: templates, reference notes, admin notes, MOCs, and static project notes.
   - Use kebab-case and type prefixes where the file is not automation-generated and not query-sensitive.
   - Do not rename generated pull notes, signal notes, or any note whose filename is part of a script, query, or report chain until dependencies are mapped.

7. Handle generated and graph-critical areas as exceptions.
   - `05_Data_Pulls/` and `06_Signals/` should remain operational output zones.
   - `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, and `10_Theses/` should only be renamed or moved after checking templates, backlinks, Dataview queries, graph assumptions, and any pull/report references.
   - If these areas need token reduction, solve it with MOCs and summaries first, not physical relocation.

8. Create archive and attachment policy.
   - Create `500-archive/` now for inactive or superseded notes.
   - Create `600-attachments/` as a future-proof destination, but it is low priority because the current vault appears to have little or no attachment sprawl.
   - Move only clearly inactive notes in phase 1; do not archive notes that are still linked from dashboards, theses, or playbooks.

9. Run a controlled migration in batches.
   - Batch A: control files + MOCs.
   - Batch B: templates and admin/reference notes.
   - Batch C: selected project notes.
   - Batch D: optional archive pass.
   - After each batch, validate links, embeds, Dataview assumptions, and scripted references.

10. Produce a migration report after each batch.
   - Renamed files.
   - Moved files.
   - Created MOCs.
   - Exclusions added.
   - Conflicts and notes intentionally left unchanged.

**What I would not do in phase 1**
- I would not collapse the whole vault into `100-admin/`, `200-projects/`, `300-references/` immediately.
- I would not rename `05_Data_Pulls/`, `06_Signals/`, `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, or `10_Theses/` until dependency analysis is complete.
- I would not move files purely for naming consistency if that breaks graph readability or automation.

**Deliverables For The First Pass**
- `_index.md`
- `AGENT.md`
- `_agent-instructions.md`
- `000-moc/` with the core MOCs
- A vault inventory and classification table
- A rename/move plan split into `safe now`, `needs dependency check`, and `do not touch`

**Decision**
The right implementation is a two-layer model:
- Layer 1: agent-efficient control files and MOCs
- Layer 2: selective structural cleanup where it does not break the existing graph and pull system

If you want, the next step should be a concrete phase-1 migration spec for this vault: exact files to create, exact exclusions, and a safe rename/move table for the first batch.

1. Add `_index.md`, `AGENT.md`, and `_agent-instructions.md`.
2. Create `000-moc/` with the core navigation notes.
3. Build a vault inventory split into `safe now`, `needs dependency check`, and `do not touch`.
4. Add exclusion rules for generated, system, and graph-heavy areas.
5. Normalize only the low-risk note classes first.

If you want, I can start implementing phase 1 now and keep the graph-critical folders untouched.

content.newText:
# AGENT

Vault operating rules:
- Read [[_index]] first.
- Open one relevant MOC before opening detailed notes.
- Prefer dashboards and reference docs before raw pull notes or graph-session outputs.
- Treat `05_Data_Pulls/` and `06_Signals/` as generated evidence, not default browsing material.
- Do not scan `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, `scripts/`, or `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/` unless the task requires them.
- Preserve link integrity and schema rules from [[04_Reference/Vault_Schemas]] and [[04_Reference/Graph Conventions]].
- Do not mass-rename or move notes in `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, `10_Theses/`, `05_Data_Pulls/`, or `06_Signals/` without dependency review.
- Keep outputs concise and structured unless a different format is requested.

---
title: Macro
type: moc
tags: [macro, regimes, indicators]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# Macro

Summary:
- Macro regimes and indicators are shared context for many theses and entity links.
- Navigate regime-first, then drill into individual indicators as needed.

Open first when:
- You need regime context, rate sensitivity, inflation context, or market-risk framing.

Key notes:
- [[00_Dashboard/Macro Regime]]
- [[09_Macro/Regimes/Rate Cut Cycle]]
- [[09_Macro/Regimes/Recession]]
- [[09_Macro/Regimes/Risk-Off]]
- [[09_Macro/Indicators/Fed Funds Rate]]
- [[09_Macro/Indicators/10Y Treasury]]

Decisions:
- Use regime notes as the main summary layer.
- Open indicator notes only when you need drivers, thresholds, or supporting evidence.

---
title: Data Sources
type: moc
tags: [data-sources, providers, pullers]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# Data Sources

Summary:
- Source metadata lives in `01_Data_Sources/`; generated evidence lives in `05_Data_Pulls/`.
- Use this map before opening raw pull notes or changing automation.

Open first when:
- You need to know which provider covers a dataset, which puller to run, or how a source note is structured.

Key notes:
- [[00_Dashboard/Data Sources Overview]]
- [[04_Reference/Pull_System_Guide]]
- [[04_Reference/Vault_Schemas]]
- [[README]]
- [[CLAUDE]]

Coverage areas:
- Macro, market data, housing and real estate, government contracts, biotech and healthcare, geospatial, legal and courts, and frontier science.

Decisions:
- Treat `01_Data_Sources/` as source metadata and `05_Data_Pulls/` as evidence snapshots.
- Check the pull guide and schema doc before changing pullers or source notes.

---
title: Entities
type: moc
tags: [entities, graph, research]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# Entities

Summary:
- Entity notes are graph hubs connecting theses to sectors, countries, commodities, ETFs, and tickers.
- Use them for context and connectivity, not as a default deep-read area.

Open first when:
- You need company, sector, country, or commodity context for a thesis or signal.

Key notes:
- [[00_Dashboard/Network Graph]]
- [[Real Estate]]
- [[Tech Sector]]
- [[USA]]
- [[Gold]]
- [[AAPL]]

Decisions:
- Preserve canonical entity names because they are graph anchors.
- Prefer selective reads from linked entities instead of scanning whole folders.

---
title: Playbooks
type: moc
tags: [playbooks, workflows, infranodus]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# Playbooks

Summary:
- Playbooks capture repeatable workflows and graph-measurement entry points.
- Generated graph sessions are evidence artifacts, not default reading material.

Open first when:
- You need a repeatable workflow, a housing-cycle runbook, or InfraNodus measurement context.

Key notes:
- [[07_Playbooks/Housing_Cycle]]
- [[00_Dashboard/InfraNodus Measurements]]
- [[04_Reference/InfraNodus Graph Measurements]]

Decisions:
- Treat `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/` as generated output.
- Open only the specific recent graph session required by the task.

---
title: Theses
type: moc
tags: [theses, synthesis, strategy]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---
# Theses

Summary:
- Thesis notes are the primary synthesis layer across macro, entity, and signal inputs.
- Use dashboards first, then open only the theses relevant to the question.

Open first when:
- You need the current investment framing, cross-domain alignment, or a thesis-specific read.

Key notes:
- [[00_Dashboard/Cross-Domain Thesis Board]]
- [[10_Theses/Housing Supply Correction]]
- [[10_Theses/AI Power Infrastructure]]
- [[10_Theses/Defense AI Autonomous Warfare]]
- [[10_Theses/Dollar Debasement Hard Money]]

Decisions:
- Thesis notes are the main synthesis surface.
- Open supporting entities, regimes, and pull notes only after the thesis note identifies the relevant links.

# Agent Instructions

Use this vault through the control layer, not by broad folder scans.

Task routing:
- Overview or current state: open [[000-moc/moc-dashboard]] first.
- Source coverage, providers, or pull commands: open [[000-moc/moc-data-sources]] and then [[04_Reference/Pull_System_Guide]] if needed.
- Schema, note rules, or graph behavior: open [[000-moc/moc-reference]].
- Regime or macro context: open [[000-moc/moc-macro]].
- Company, sector, country, or commodity context: open [[000-moc/moc-entities]].
- Thesis synthesis: open [[000-moc/moc-theses]].
- Playbook execution or graph measurements: open [[000-moc/moc-playbooks]].

Default exclusions:
- `.obsidian/`
- `.claude/`
- `scripts/`
- `05_Data_Pulls/`
- `06_Signals/`
- `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/`

Working rules:
- Load the minimum number of notes required to answer the task.
- Prefer dashboards, MOCs, and reference notes before detailed evidence notes.
- Open raw pull notes only when you need provenance, time series detail, or signal context.
- Open graph-session notes only when the task is specifically about InfraNodus output.
- Preserve canonical entity, regime, and thesis names because they anchor graph links and dashboards.
- Preserve dated filenames in generated areas.
- For new admin or navigation notes, prefer short summaries, lean frontmatter, and direct links.

Phase-1 migration boundary:
- Safe to normalize first: root control notes, `00_Dashboard/`, `03_Templates/`, `04_Reference/`, and future admin notes.
- Needs dependency review before rename or move: `01_Data_Sources/`, `07_Playbooks/Housing_Cycle.md`, `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, `10_Theses/`.
- Do not touch without an explicit migration step: `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, `scripts/`, `05_Data_Pulls/`, `06_Signals/`, `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/`.

---

# Reference

Summary:
- Core operating docs for schemas, pull workflows, graph conventions, and InfraNodus usage.

Open first when:

Key notes:
- [[04_Reference/Vault_Schemas]]
- [[04_Reference/Pull_System_Guide]]
- [[04_Reference/Graph Conventions]]
- [[04_Reference/InfraNodus Graph Measurements]]

- Schema rules govern edits.
- Graph conventions govern link structure.
- InfraNodus materials are read on demand, not default context.

Default exclusions:
- Do not scan `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, or `scripts/` unless the task is explicitly about tooling or configuration.
- Do not load `05_Data_Pulls/`, `06_Signals/`, or `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/` by default. Open them only when you need evidence, provenance, or a recent generated output.
- Treat `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, and `10_Theses/` as graph-critical areas. Prefer the MOCs first, then open only the specific notes needed for the task.

- Fastest entry point for current vault state.

# Phase 1 Vault Inventory

Summary:
- This inventory defines the safe phase-1 boundary for token-efficiency cleanup.
- The goal is to add a control layer first and avoid breaking dashboards, graph links, or automation.

| Area | Markdown notes |
| --- | ---: |
| `00_Dashboard/` | 8 |
| `01_Data_Sources/` | 68 |
| `02_Projects/` | 0 |
| `03_Templates/` | 11 |
| `04_Reference/` | 4 |
| `05_Data_Pulls/` | 99 |
| `06_Signals/` | 9 |
| `07_Playbooks/` | 4 |
| `08_Entities/` | 74 |
| `09_Macro/` | 26 |
| `10_Theses/` | 18 |

Classification:

| Area | Status | Phase 1 handling | Rationale |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Root control files | safe now | create and maintain | Adds a low-token entrypoint without changing graph structure. |
| `000-moc/` | safe now | create and expand | Provides MOC-first navigation over the current domain folders. |
| `100-admin/` | safe now | create and use for migration docs | Keeps operational notes separate from research content. |
| `00_Dashboard/` | safe now | normalize later | Small, high-value overview surface. |
| `03_Templates/` | safe now | normalize later | Stable note templates with low graph risk. |
| `04_Reference/` | safe now | normalize later | Core schema and workflow docs. |
| `02_Projects/` | safe now | hold | Currently empty. |
| `01_Data_Sources/` | dependency check | review before rename or move | Source metadata may be referenced by scripts, dashboards, and schema rules. |
| `07_Playbooks/Housing_Cycle.md` | dependency check | review before rename or move | Active workflow note may be tied to playbook execution conventions. |
| `08_Entities/` | dependency check | preserve names in phase 1 | Canonical entity names anchor graph links and dashboards. |
| `09_Macro/` | dependency check | preserve names in phase 1 | Regime and indicator names are link targets across the vault. |
| `10_Theses/` | dependency check | preserve names in phase 1 | Thesis names and links are central to synthesis and dashboards. |
| `05_Data_Pulls/` | do not touch | exclude by default | Generated evidence notes; dated filenames are intentional. |
| `06_Signals/` | do not touch | exclude by default | Generated event notes feeding alert workflows. |
| `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/` | do not touch | exclude by default | Generated InfraNodus outputs. |
| `scripts/` | do not touch | exclude by default | Operational code, not note-browsing context. |
| `.obsidian/` | do not touch | exclude by default | App and plugin configuration. |
| `.claude/` | do not touch | exclude by default | Tooling state, not vault knowledge content. |

Immediate next steps:
- Use [[_index]] as the first read for agent work.
- Use the MOCs in `000-moc/` before opening detailed notes.
- Normalize `00_Dashboard/`, `03_Templates/`, and `04_Reference/` before touching graph-core areas.
- Produce a note-level rename and move table only after dependency review for data sources, entities, macro, and theses.

# Agent Instructions

Use this vault through the control layer, not by broad folder scans.

Task routing:
- Overview or current state: open [[000-moc/moc-dashboard]] first.
- Source coverage, providers, or pull commands: open [[000-moc/moc-data-sources]] and then [[04_Reference/Pull_System_Guide]] if needed.
- Schema, note rules, or graph behavior: open [[000-moc/moc-reference]].
- Regime or macro context: open [[000-moc/moc-macro]].
- Company, sector, country, or commodity context: open [[000-moc/moc-entities]].
- Thesis synthesis: open [[000-moc/moc-theses]].
- Playbook execution or graph measurements: open [[000-moc/moc-playbooks]].

Default exclusions:
- `.obsidian/`
- `.claude/`
- `scripts/`
- `05_Data_Pulls/`
- `06_Signals/`
- `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/`

Working rules:
- Load the minimum number of notes required to answer the task.
- Prefer dashboards, MOCs, and reference notes before detailed evidence notes.
- Open raw pull notes only when you need provenance, time series detail, or signal context.
- Open graph-session notes only when the task is specifically about InfraNodus output.
- Preserve canonical entity, regime, and thesis names because they anchor graph links and dashboards.
- Preserve dated filenames in generated areas.
- For new admin or navigation notes, prefer short summaries, lean frontmatter, and direct links.

Phase-1 migration boundary:
- Safe to normalize first: root control notes, `00_Dashboard/`, `03_Templates/`, `04_Reference/`, and future admin notes.
- Needs dependency review before rename or move: `01_Data_Sources/`, `07_Playbooks/Housing_Cycle.md`, `08_Entities/`, `09_Macro/`, `10_Theses/`.
- Do not touch without an explicit migration step: `.obsidian/`, `.claude/`, `scripts/`, `05_Data_Pulls/`, `06_Signals/`, `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/`.

---
title: Graph Conventions
type: reference
tags: [reference, conventions, graph]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---

# Network Graph Conventions

Summary:
- Link protocol for the three-layer research graph: macro, entities, and theses.
- Read this before renaming graph nodes, changing wiki links, or editing graph-facing templates.

## Architecture

The network graph system organizes investment knowledge into three layers:

```text
Layer 1: Macro (09_Macro/)
  Regimes -> Indicators
  e.g., [[Stagflation]] -> [[CPI]], [[PMI]], [[GDP Growth]]

Layer 2: Entities (08_Entities/)
  Sectors, Countries, Commodities, Stocks, ETFs, Currencies
  e.g., [[Tech Sector]] -> [[AAPL]], [[USA]], [[Lithium]]

Layer 3: Theses (10_Theses/)
  Trade ideas linking entities + macro
  e.g., "Energy Supercycle" -> [[Oil]], [[XOM]], [[Saudi Arabia]], [[Inflationary Boom]]
```

## How Graph Edges Are Created

### 1. Frontmatter Wiki Links

```yaml
bullish_drivers: ["[[Rate Cut Cycle]]", "[[Weak USD]]"]
bearish_drivers: ["[[Recession]]", "[[China Slowdown]]"]
related_entities: ["[[AAPL]]", "[[Tech Sector]]"]
sector: "[[Tech Sector]]"
country: "[[USA]]"
```

These create real graph edges and stay queryable in Dataview.

### 2. Inline Wiki Links

```markdown
## Bullish Factors
- [[Rate Cut Cycle]] lowers discount rates, boosting growth valuations
- [[AI Integration]] drives Services revenue growth
```

Use inline links for narrative context around a note's main relationships.

### 3. Directional Tags

```text
#bullish-for/AAPL
#bearish-for/XOM
```

Use these sparingly for lightweight sentiment tagging when a full note would be excessive.

### 4. Bridge Notes

For complex multi-entity relationships, create a thesis note in `10_Theses/` that links all related entities, regimes, and indicators together.

## Frontmatter Schema

Every note in the network should have:

| Field | Purpose | Example |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `node_type` | Identifies the note type | `stock`, `sector`, `regime` |
| `status` | Current relevance | `Active`, `Watching`, `Archived` |
| `bullish_drivers` | What helps this entity | `["[[Rate Cut Cycle]]"]` |
| `bearish_drivers` | What hurts this entity | `["[[Recession]]"]` |
| `related_entities` | Related nodes | `["[[AAPL]]", "[[Tech Sector]]"]` |
| `tags` | Categorization | `[stock]`, `[macro, regime]` |

## Graph View Color Legend

| Color | Node Type | Folder |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Blue | Stocks | `08_Entities/Stocks/` |
| Light Blue | ETFs | `08_Entities/ETFs/` |
| Green | Sectors | `08_Entities/Sectors/` |
| Orange | Countries | `08_Entities/Countries/` |
| Gold | Commodities | `08_Entities/Commodities/` |
| Teal | Currencies | `08_Entities/Currencies/` |
| Purple | Macro Regimes | `09_Macro/Regimes/` |
| Light Purple | Macro Indicators | `09_Macro/Indicators/` |
| Magenta | Theses | `10_Theses/` |

## Adding New Nodes

1. Use the appropriate template from `03_Templates/`.
2. Fill in the frontmatter, especially `bullish_drivers`, `bearish_drivers`, and `related_entities`.
3. Add inline wiki links in the body for narrative context.
4. Check the graph view to verify connections appear.

## Bidirectional Linking

When linking entity A to entity B, try to add the link in both notes:

- `AAPL.md` -> `sector: "[[Tech Sector]]"`
- `Tech Sector.md` -> `key_stocks: ["[[AAPL]]"]`

This creates stronger graph edges and ensures both notes show backlinks.

## Macro Hierarchy

Tier 1, regimes:
- `key_indicators` links down to indicators.

Tier 2, indicators:
- `parent_regimes` links up to regimes.
- `affects_sectors` and `affects_commodities` link across to entities.

## Templates Available

| Template | Location | Use For |
|----------|----------|---------|
| Stock | `03_Templates/Stock.md` | Individual equities |
| Sector | `03_Templates/Sector.md` | Sector notes |
| Country | `03_Templates/Country.md` | National economies |
| Commodity | `03_Templates/Commodity.md` | Raw materials |
| Macro Regime | `03_Templates/Macro_Regime.md` | Market environments |
| Macro Indicator | `03_Templates/Macro_Indicator.md` | Economic data points |
| Thesis | `03_Templates/Thesis.md` | Trade ideas |

## InfraNodus-Ready Notes

- Use canonical `[[Wiki Links]]` in both frontmatter and body text.
- Keep one observation per paragraph or bullet when possible.
- Add a short summary above large tables so concept extraction has readable text.
- Prefer bridge notes in `10_Theses/` when connecting multiple entities and macro regimes.
- Save measurement takeaways in a Graph Session note so graph findings become durable notes.
content.oldText:
---
tags: [reference, conventions, graph]
---

# Network Graph Conventions

## Architecture

The network graph system organizes investment knowledge into three layers:

```text
Layer 1: Macro (09_Macro/)
  Regimes -> Indicators
  e.g., [[Stagflation]] -> [[CPI]], [[PMI]], [[GDP Growth]]

Layer 2: Entities (08_Entities/)
  Sectors, Countries, Commodities, Stocks, ETFs, Currencies
  e.g., [[Tech Sector]] -> [[AAPL]], [[USA]], [[Lithium]]

Layer 3: Theses (10_Theses/)
  Trade ideas linking entities + macro
  e.g., "Energy Supercycle" -> [[Oil]], [[XOM]], [[Saudi Arabia]], [[Inflationary Boom]]
```

## How Graph Edges Are Created

### 1. Frontmatter Wiki Links

```yaml
bullish_drivers: ["[[Rate Cut Cycle]]", "[[Weak USD]]"]
bearish_drivers: ["[[Recession]]", "[[China Slowdown]]"]
related_entities: ["[[AAPL]]", "[[Tech Sector]]"]
sector: "[[Tech Sector]]"
country: "[[USA]]"
```

These create real graph edges and stay queryable in Dataview.

### 2. Inline Wiki Links

```markdown
## Bullish Factors
- [[Rate Cut Cycle]] lowers discount rates, boosting growth valuations
- [[AI Integration]] drives Services revenue growth
```

Use inline links for narrative context around a note's main relationships.

### 3. Directional Tags

```text
#bullish-for/AAPL
#bearish-for/XOM
```

Use these sparingly for lightweight sentiment tagging when a full note would be excessive.

### 4. Bridge Notes

For complex multi-entity relationships, create a thesis note in `10_Theses/` that links all related entities, regimes, and indicators together.

## Frontmatter Schema

Every note in the network should have:

| Field | Purpose | Example |
|-------|---------|---------|
| `node_type` | Identifies the note type | `stock`, `sector`, `regime` |
| `status` | Current relevance | `Active`, `Watching`, `Archived` |
| `bullish_drivers` | What helps this entity | `["[[Rate Cut Cycle]]"]` |
| `bearish_drivers` | What hurts this entity | `["[[Recession]]"]` |
| `related_entities` | Related nodes | `["[[AAPL]]", "[[Tech Sector]]"]` |
| `tags` | Categorization | `[stock]`, `[macro, regime]` |

## Graph View Color Legend

| Color | Node Type | Folder |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Blue | Stocks | `08_Entities/Stocks/` |
| Light Blue | ETFs | `08_Entities/ETFs/` |
| Green | Sectors | `08_Entities/Sectors/` |
| Orange | Countries | `08_Entities/Countries/` |
| Gold | Commodities | `08_Entities/Commodities/` |
| Teal | Currencies | `08_Entities/Currencies/` |
| Purple | Macro Regimes | `09_Macro/Regimes/` |
| Light Purple | Macro Indicators | `09_Macro/Indicators/` |
| Magenta | Theses | `10_Theses/` |

## Adding New Nodes

1. Use the appropriate template from `03_Templates/`.
2. Fill in the frontmatter, especially `bullish_drivers`, `bearish_drivers`, and `related_entities`.
3. Add inline wiki links in the body for narrative context.
4. Check the graph view to verify connections appear.

## Bidirectional Linking

When linking entity A to entity B, try to add the link in both notes:

- `AAPL.md` -> `sector: "[[Tech Sector]]"`
- `Tech Sector.md` -> `key_stocks: ["[[AAPL]]"]`

This creates stronger graph edges and ensures both notes show backlinks.

## Macro Hierarchy

Tier 1, regimes:
- `key_indicators` links down to indicators.

Tier 2, indicators:
- `parent_regimes` links up to regimes.
- `affects_sectors` and `affects_commodities` link across to entities.

## Templates Available

| Template | Location | Use For |
|----------|----------|---------|
| Stock | `03_Templates/Stock.md` | Individual equities |
| Sector | `03_Templates/Sector.md` | Sector notes |
| Country | `03_Templates/Country.md` | National economies |
| Commodity | `03_Templates/Commodity.md` | Raw materials |
| Macro Regime | `03_Templates/Macro_Regime.md` | Market environments |
| Macro Indicator | `03_Templates/Macro_Indicator.md` | Economic data points |
| Thesis | `03_Templates/Thesis.md` | Trade ideas |

## InfraNodus-Ready Notes

- Use canonical `[[Wiki Links]]` in both frontmatter and body text.
- Keep one observation per paragraph or bullet when possible.
- Add a short summary above large tables so concept extraction has readable text.
- Prefer bridge notes in `10_Theses/` when connecting multiple entities and macro regimes.
- Save measurement takeaways in a Graph Session note so graph findings become durable notes.

# Macro Regime Dashboard

Summary:
- Current macro evidence board for rates, labor, inflation, growth, and related news.
- Start here when you need regime context before opening specific indicators or pull notes.

## Latest Macro Data

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  source AS "Source",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  choice(signal_status = "critical", "🔴",
    choice(signal_status = "alert", "🟠",
      choice(signal_status = "watch", "🟡", "⚪"))) AS "Signal"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Macro"
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 15
```

## Rate Environment

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  signal_status AS "Status"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Macro"
WHERE contains(tags, "rates") OR contains(tags, "yields") OR contains(tags, "treasury")
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 5
```

## Labor Market

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  signal_status AS "Status"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Macro"
WHERE contains(tags, "labor") OR contains(tags, "employment") OR contains(tags, "unemployment")
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 5
```

## Inflation

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  signal_status AS "Status"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Macro"
WHERE contains(tags, "inflation") OR contains(tags, "cpi") OR contains(tags, "pce")
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 5
```

## GDP & Income

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date_pulled AS "Date",
  signal_status AS "Status"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Macro"
WHERE contains(tags, "gdp") OR contains(tags, "income") OR contains(tags, "bea")
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 5
```

## News Sentiment

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  topic AS "Topic",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE contains(tags, "news") OR contains(tags, "newsapi")
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 5
```

## Key Questions

- **Yield curve shape?** → Check Rate Environment section
- **Labor tight or loosening?** → Check Labor Market section
- **Inflation hot or anchored?** → Check Inflation section
- **Growth accelerating or slowing?** → Check GDP & Income section

title: Vault Schemas
type: reference
tags: [reference, schema, validation]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---

# Vault Schemas

Summary:
- Canonical frontmatter rules for source notes, pull notes, entities, theses, and macro notes.
- Read this before changing schemas, validator logic, or note templates.

## Pull Note Schema

Required fields for notes in `05_Data_Pulls/`:

| Field | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `title` | Human-readable note title |
| `source` | Data provider or API name |
| `date_pulled` | Pull date in `YYYY-MM-DD` |
| `domain` | Lowercase domain such as `macro`, `government`, `market`, `housing`, `biotech`, `research`, `energy` |
| `data_type` | Note type such as `snapshot`, `event_list`, `time_series`, `8k_filings` |
| `frequency` | Pull cadence or data cadence |
| `signal_status` | `clear`, `watch`, `alert`, or `critical` |
| `signals` | Array of fired signals, or `[]` |
| `tags` | Array of tags for Dataview and filtering |

Rules:

- Pull notes should live directly under a first-level folder in `05_Data_Pulls/`.
- Filenames should be date-stamped and path-safe: `YYYY-MM-DD_Name.md`.
- Pull notes may add extra fields like `topic`, `symbol`, `record_date`, `lookback_days`, or `related_pulls`.

## Data Source Schema

Required fields for notes in `01_Data_Sources/`:

| Field | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `name` | Display name |
| `category` | Must match the top-level folder |
| `type` | Usually `API`, `Dataset`, or `Feed` |
| `provider` | Data owner or publisher |
| `pricing` | `Free`, `Freemium`, `Paid`, or similar |
| `status` | `Active`, `Watching`, `Archived` |
| `priority` | Relative importance |
| `url` | Primary source URL |
| `provides` | Array of data types or outputs |
| `best_use_cases` | Array of intended uses |
| `tags` | Array of tags |
| `related_sources` | Array of related source notes |
| `key_location` | Env var or `None required` |
| `integrated` | Boolean |
| `notes` | Short summary |

Recommended fields for integrated sources:

| Field | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `linked_puller` | Matching CLI puller name |
| `update_frequency` | Expected refresh cadence |
| `owner` | Human owner of the note or integration |
| `last_reviewed` | Last schema/content review date |

## Source Categories

The `category` field should match the source folder exactly:

- `Biotech_Healthcare`
- `Climate_Energy`
- `Developer_Code`
- `Frontier_Science`
- `Fundamentals`
- `Geospatial`
- `Government_Contracts`
- `Housing_Real_Estate`
- `Legal_Courts`
- `Macro`
- `Market_Data`
- `News_Media`
- `Prediction_Markets`
- `Private_Markets_VC`
- `Social_Sentiment`
- `Supply_Chain_Trade`

## Validation

Run the validator from `scripts/`:

```powershell
node run.mjs validate
```

The validator checks:

- Required frontmatter on source notes and pull notes
- Source note category alignment with folders
- Boolean `integrated` values
- Valid `signal_status` values
- Pull note layout issues such as nested files under `05_Data_Pulls/`

tags: [dashboard, network, graph, infranodus]
last_updated: 2026-03-27

# Network Graph Dashboard

Summary:
- High-level graph status for entities, regimes, indicators, theses, and bridge candidates.
- Use this before opening large graph-heavy folders or running InfraNodus.

Use [[InfraNodus Measurements]] when you want cluster, gap, and bridge analysis instead of a raw Obsidian graph. Use [[InfraNodus Graph Measurements]] for the measurement workflow and plugin defaults.

## All Entities
```dataview
TABLE node_type AS "Type", status, length(bullish_drivers) AS "Bull", length(bearish_drivers) AS "Bear"
FROM "08_Entities" OR "09_Macro" OR "10_Theses"
WHERE node_type
SORT node_type ASC, file.name ASC
```

## Active Macro Regimes
```dataview
TABLE confidence, length(key_indicators) AS "Indicators", length(favors_sectors) AS "Favors", length(hurts_sectors) AS "Hurts"
FROM "09_Macro/Regimes"
WHERE status = "Active"
SORT confidence DESC
```

## Stocks by Sector
```dataview
TABLE ticker, sector, country, status
FROM "08_Entities/Stocks"
SORT sector ASC, ticker ASC
```

## Commodities Overview
```dataview
TABLE commodity_type AS "Type", length(key_countries) AS "Countries", length(key_sectors) AS "Sectors"
FROM "08_Entities/Commodities"
SORT commodity_type ASC
```

## Macro Indicators by Category
```dataview
TABLE frequency, source, trend, length(parent_regimes) AS "Regimes"
FROM "09_Macro/Indicators"
SORT file.name ASC
```

## Bridge Candidates
```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Node",
  node_type AS "Type",
  length(bullish_drivers) AS "Bull",
  length(bearish_drivers) AS "Bear",
  length(related_entities) AS "Related"
FROM "08_Entities" OR "09_Macro" OR "10_Theses"
WHERE node_type AND (length(bullish_drivers) > 0 OR length(bearish_drivers) > 0) AND length(related_entities) > 0
SORT length(related_entities) DESC, length(bullish_drivers) DESC, length(bearish_drivers) DESC
LIMIT 20
```

## Orphan Detection (Low Connectivity)
```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Node",
  node_type AS "Type"
FROM "08_Entities" OR "09_Macro" OR "10_Theses"
WHERE node_type AND length(bullish_drivers) = 0 AND length(bearish_drivers) = 0 AND length(related_entities) = 0
SORT node_type ASC
```

## How To Use

- Open the local Obsidian graph when you want visual sanity-checks.
- Open [[InfraNodus Measurements]] when you want graph measurements and gap detection.
- Open one folder at a time in InfraNodus for cleaner results.

# Pull System Guide

Summary:
- Reference for the current CLI surface in `scripts/run.mjs`.
- Use this for command syntax, the morning sequence, and playbook entry points.

## Quick Start

Run pulls from the scripts directory in PowerShell:

```powershell
Set-Location "C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\scripts"
node run.mjs help
node run.mjs status
node run.mjs <puller> [flags]
```

The CLI loads `.env` from the vault root:

```text
C:\Users\CaveUser\Documents\Obsidian Vault\My_Data\.env
```

## Daily Workflow

1. Run `node run.mjs status` to confirm keys before starting.
2. Run the morning sequence line by line, or use the external `morning-data-pull` scheduled task if it exists on this machine.
3. Run targeted thesis stacks or a playbook when you need a deeper read.
4. Review notes in `05_Data_Pulls/` and signal notes in `06_Signals/`.

## Morning Sequence (17 Commands)

Copy-paste safe for PowerShell 5.1:

```powershell
# Macro and market
node run.mjs fred --group rates
node run.mjs fred --group housing
node run.mjs fred --group labor
node run.mjs alphavantage --quote SPY
node run.mjs treasury --yields

# Government and regulatory
node run.mjs openfema --recent
node run.mjs usaspending --recent
node run.mjs fda --recent-approvals
node run.mjs sec --thesis

# Clinical and patent intelligence
node run.mjs clinicaltrials --oncology
node run.mjs clinicaltrials --amr
node run.mjs uspto --ptab

# Research sources
node run.mjs arxiv --drones
node run.mjs arxiv --amr
node run.mjs pubmed --amr
node run.mjs pubmed --psychedelics

# News
node run.mjs newsapi --topic business
```

## Utilities and Playbooks

```powershell
node run.mjs help
node run.mjs status
node run.mjs playbook housing-cycle
```

Only `housing-cycle` is currently implemented.

## Morning Pullers

| Puller | Key | Output | Common flags |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fred` | `FRED_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Macro/` or `05_Data_Pulls/Housing/` | `--group housing, labor, inflation, rates, credit, liquidity`, `--series`, `--limit` |
| `alphavantage` | `ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Market/` | `--quote`, `--overview` |
| `treasury` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Macro/` | `--yields` |
| `openfema` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Government/` | `--recent` |
| `usaspending` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Government/` | `--recent` |
| `fda` | Optional `FDA_OPEN_DATA_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Biotech/` | `--recent-approvals` |
| `sec` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Government/` | `--thesis`, thesis flags, `--sectors [name]` |
| `clinicaltrials` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Biotech/` | `--oncology`, `--cardio`, `--neuro`, `--amr`, `--glp1`, `--geneediting`, `--alzheimers`, `--longevity`, `--query` |
| `uspto` | `PATENTSVIEW_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Government/` | `--ptab`, `--filings`, `--all` |
| `arxiv` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Research/` | `--drones`, `--defense`, `--amr`, `--psychedelics`, `--glp1`, `--geneediting`, `--alzheimers`, `--longevity`, `--nuclear`, `--quantum`, `--humanoid`, `--space`, `--all` |
| `pubmed` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Research/` | `--amr`, `--psychedelics`, `--glp1`, `--geneediting`, `--alzheimers`, `--longevity`, `--all` |
| `newsapi` | `NEWSAPI_API_KEY` | Topic-based domain under `05_Data_Pulls/` | `--topic`, `--limit` |

## Additional Pullers

These are implemented in the repo but are not part of the default morning sequence.

| Puller | Key | Output | Common flags |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fmp` | `FINANCIAL_MODELING_PREP_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Market/` | `--profile`, `--income`, `--options` |
| `bea` | `BEA_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Macro/` | `--gdp`, `--income` |
| `eia` | `EIA_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Energy/` | `--electricity-demand`, `--generation-mix`, `--regional-load`, `--all` |
| `cboe` | None | `05_Data_Pulls/Market/` | `--skew`, `--vix`, `--all` |
| `sam` | `SAM_GOV_API_KEY` | `05_Data_Pulls/Government/` | `--entities`, `--opportunities`, `--all` |
| `socrata` | Optional `SOCRATA_APP_TOKEN` | Dataset-based domain under `05_Data_Pulls/` | `--permits`, `--311`, `--chi-permits`, `--custom` |

## SEC Coverage

### Thesis Flags

| Flag | Theme |
|---|---|
| `--drones` | Drone autonomous systems |
| `--defense` | Defense AI and autonomous warfare |
| `--amr` | Antimicrobial resistance |
| `--psychedelics` | Psychedelic mental health |
| `--glp1` | GLP-1 and metabolic disease |
| `--geneediting` | Gene editing and CRISPR |
| `--alzheimers` | Alzheimer's disease modification |
| `--longevity` | Longevity and aging biology |
| `--nuclear` | Nuclear renaissance and SMRs |
| `--storage` | Grid-scale battery storage |
| `--aipower` | AI power infrastructure |
| `--humanoid` | Humanoid robotics |
| `--quantum` | Quantum computing |
| `--semis` | Semiconductor sovereignty and CHIPS |
| `--housing` | Housing supply correction |
| `--hardmoney` | Dollar debasement and hard money |
| `--space` | Space domain awareness |
| `--hypersonics` | Hypersonic weapons and advanced defense |

### Sector Flags

```powershell
node run.mjs sec --sectors
node run.mjs sec --sectors tech
node run.mjs sec --sectors health
node run.mjs sec --sectors energy
node run.mjs sec --sectors finance
node run.mjs sec --sectors industrial
node run.mjs sec --sectors consumer
node run.mjs sec --sectors realestate
node run.mjs sec --sectors clean
```

### 8-K Items Tracked

| Item | Event |
|---|---|
| `1.01` | Material contract |
| `1.02` | Contract termination |
| `2.02` | Earnings results |
| `2.06` | Material impairment |
| `4.01` | Auditor change |
| `5.02` | Executive departure |
| `5.03` | Amendment to articles |
| `7.01` | Regulation FD disclosure |
| `8.01` | Other material event |

## Representative Output Layout

Exact filenames are date-stamped by the pullers. Use this as a directory map, not a hardcoded file list.

```text
05_Data_Pulls/
  Macro/       FRED_Rates, FRED_Labor, Treasury_Rates, BEA_*, News_* for macro topics
  Market/      AV_Quote_*, AV_Overview_*, FMP_*, CBOE_*
  Government/  OpenFEMA_*, USASpending_*, USPTO_*, SEC_*, SAM_*, Socrata_311, Socrata_Custom
  Biotech/     FDA_*, ClinicalTrials_*, News_* for biotech topics
  Research/    arXiv_*, PubMed_*
  Housing/     FRED_Housing, Housing_Cycle_Report, Socrata_permits, Socrata_chi-permits, News_* for housing topics
  Energy/      EIA_*, News_* for energy topics

06_Signals/
  signals_* notes written by pullers that evaluate thresholds
```

`newsapi`, `fred`, `eia`, and `socrata` route output by topic or domain, so the final folder depends on the flags you use.

## API Keys

| Key variable | Used by |
|---|---|
| `FRED_API_KEY` | `fred` |
| `ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY` | `alphavantage` |
| `NEWSAPI_API_KEY` | `newsapi` |
| `PATENTSVIEW_API_KEY` | `uspto` |
| `BEA_API_KEY` | `bea` |
| `EIA_API_KEY` | `eia` |
| `FINANCIAL_MODELING_PREP_API_KEY` | `fmp` |
| `SAM_GOV_API_KEY` | `sam` |
| `SOCRATA_APP_TOKEN` | `socrata` |
| `FDA_OPEN_DATA_API_KEY` | Optional for `fda` |

No key is required for `treasury`, `openfema`, `usaspending`, `clinicaltrials`, `arxiv`, `pubmed`, `sec`, and `cboe`.

## PowerShell-Safe Stacks

Run these as separate lines in PowerShell 5.1:

```powershell
# AMR stack
node run.mjs clinicaltrials --amr
node run.mjs pubmed --amr
node run.mjs sec --amr
```

```powershell
# Housing stack
node run.mjs fred --group housing
node run.mjs fred --group rates
node run.mjs sec --housing
node run.mjs playbook housing-cycle
```

```powershell
# Defense stack
node run.mjs arxiv --drones
node run.mjs sec --drones
node run.mjs sec --hypersonics
node run.mjs usaspending --recent
```

```powershell
# Psychedelics stack
node run.mjs pubmed --psychedelics
node run.mjs clinicaltrials --neuro
node run.mjs sec --psychedelics
```

## Known Limitations

| Item | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `morning-data-pull` task | External | This repo references the scheduled task but does not define it. |
| Playbooks | Limited | `housing-cycle` exists. No `recession-watch` file is present. |
| BLS | Not wired | `BLS_API_KEY` exists in config, but there is no `bls.mjs` puller. |
| USPTO full-text migration | In progress | `uspto --filings` still depends on the current PatentsView migration path. |
| SEC exhibits | Partial | The SEC puller tracks filing metadata and item codes, not Exhibit 99.1 text. |
| NewsAPI topic matching | Narrow | The puller maps the first word of `--topic` to a category on `top-headlines`. |
| Socrata token | Optional | The puller runs without `SOCRATA_APP_TOKEN`, but at lower rate limits. |

## Source of Truth

For future updates, keep these in sync:

1. `scripts/run.mjs` help text
2. `scripts/pullers/*.mjs` flag handling and output paths
3. This guide

# Cross-Domain Thesis Board

Summary:
- Cross-domain signal board for thesis-level convergence.
- Start here when you need to see where housing, macro, biotech, government, and market evidence are lining up.

## Active Signals Across Domains

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  choice(signal_status = "critical", "🔴",
    choice(signal_status = "alert", "🟠",
      choice(signal_status = "watch", "🟡", "⚪"))) AS "",
  file.link AS "Report",
  domain AS "Domain",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE signal_status != "clear" AND signal_status != null
SORT date_pulled DESC
```

## Housing + Macro Convergence

Is macro pressure affecting housing?

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  domain AS "Domain",
  signal_status AS "Signal",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE (domain = "housing" OR domain = "macro") AND signal_status != "clear" AND signal_status != null
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Government + Disaster Risk

Where is federal spending going? Are disasters affecting housing markets?

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  source AS "Source",
  signal_status AS "Signal",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Government"
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Biotech Pipeline

Recent FDA activity and healthcare news.

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  source AS "Source",
  signal_status AS "Signal",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Biotech"
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Market Snapshots

Latest equity data and company profiles.

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  source AS "Source",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls/Market"
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Cross-Domain Thesis Templates

| Thesis | Data Sources | Key Question |
|--------|-------------|--------------|
| **Housing Cycle** | FRED housing + rates + labor, OpenFEMA | Is housing expanding, peaking, cooling, or contracting? |
| **Recession Watch** | FRED rates + labor + credit, Treasury, BEA GDP | What's the recession probability over next 12 months? |
| **Biotech Catalyst** | FDA approvals, NewsAPI health | Which therapy areas are seeing concentrated approval activity? |
| **GovCon Pipeline** | USASpending, SAM.gov | Which companies are winning large federal contracts? |
| **Regional Risk** | OpenFEMA + housing + Socrata permits | Which metros have elevated disaster risk AND rising housing? |
| **Energy Transition** | EIA, NOAA storms | Is energy supply/demand shifting? Are storms disrupting infrastructure? |

## How to Read This Board

1. **Check Active Signals** — any 🔴 or 🟠 requires immediate attention
2. **Check Convergence** — signals in multiple domains pointing the same direction = high conviction
3. **Check Freshness** — stale data means blind spots. See [[Data Freshness]] dashboard
4. **Run Playbooks** — use `node run.mjs playbook housing-cycle` for automated multi-source analysis

title: InfraNodus Measurements

tags: [dashboard, graph, infranodus]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---

# InfraNodus Measurements

Summary:
- Entry point for graph measurement work across entities, macro, theses, sources, and evidence folders.
- Open this before running InfraNodus so scope choice and follow-up actions stay consistent.

Use this dashboard as the entry point for graph measurement work in the vault. For setup and interpretation rules, see [[InfraNodus Graph Measurements]]. Capture findings with the `Graph Session` template.

## Recommended Scopes

| Scope | Open in InfraNodus | Best modes | Main question |
|-------|--------------------|------------|---------------|
| `08_Entities/` | Folder | Topics, Gaps | Which assets cluster together, and which bridges are missing? |
| `09_Macro/` | Folder | Topics, Concepts | Are regimes and indicators forming one system or several islands? |
| `10_Theses/` | Folder | Topics, Gaps | Which theses overlap, and which themes need bridge notes? |
| `05_Data_Pulls/<domain>/` | Folder | Trends, Topics | What changed inside one evidence stream recently? |
| `01_Data_Sources/` | Folder | Topics, Gaps | Where does the source catalog overlap, and where are the blind spots? |

## Hub Candidates

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Node",
  node_type AS "Type",
  length(bullish_drivers) AS "Bull",
  length(bearish_drivers) AS "Bear",
  length(related_entities) AS "Related"
FROM "08_Entities" OR "09_Macro" OR "10_Theses"
WHERE node_type AND (length(bullish_drivers) > 0 OR length(bearish_drivers) > 0 OR length(related_entities) > 0)
SORT length(related_entities) DESC, length(bullish_drivers) DESC, length(bearish_drivers) DESC
LIMIT 25
```

## Bridge Candidate Theses

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Thesis",
  length(core_entities) AS "Core",
  length(supporting_regimes) AS "Regimes",
  length(key_indicators) AS "Indicators"
FROM "10_Theses"
WHERE length(core_entities) > 1 OR length(supporting_regimes) > 1 OR length(key_indicators) > 1
SORT length(core_entities) DESC, length(supporting_regimes) DESC, length(key_indicators) DESC
LIMIT 20
```

## Low Connectivity Queue

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Node",
  node_type AS "Type"
FROM "08_Entities" OR "09_Macro" OR "10_Theses"
WHERE node_type AND length(bullish_drivers) = 0 AND length(bearish_drivers) = 0 AND length(related_entities) = 0
SORT node_type ASC, file.name ASC
```

## Recent Signal-Rich Pulls

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Pull",
  domain AS "Domain",
  source AS "Source",
  signal_status AS "Signal",
  date_pulled AS "Date"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE signal_status = "critical" OR signal_status = "alert" OR signal_status = "watch"
SORT date_pulled DESC
LIMIT 20
```

## Recent Graph Sessions

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Session",
  scope AS "Scope",
  cluster_count AS "Topics",
  gap_count AS "Gaps",
  date AS "Date"
FROM "07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions"
SORT date DESC, file.name DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Working Rules

- Start in `topics`, move to `gaps`, then check `graph` for structural sanity.
- Measure one folder at a time. Use a thesis note when you need a cross-folder graph.
- Save takeaways in a Graph Session note instead of leaving them only in the plugin view.
- Favor notes with explicit `[[Wiki Links]]`; InfraNodus reads those better than implied relationships.

# Housing Intelligence Dashboard

Summary:
- Housing entrypoint for source coverage, key indicators, and recent reports.
- Use this before opening national or metro source folders directly.

## Housing Data Sources

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Source",
  pricing AS "Pricing",
  priority AS "Priority",
  provides AS "Key Data",
  integrated AS "Live?"
FROM "01_Data_Sources/Housing_Real_Estate"
SORT priority ASC, name ASC
```

## Key National Indicators

| Indicator | Primary Source | Update Frequency |
|-----------|---------------|-----------------|
| Case-Shiller Home Price Index | [[FRED Housing Series]] | Monthly (2-month lag) |
| Housing Starts & Permits | [[Census Bureau Housing Data]] | Monthly |
| FHFA House Price Index | [[FHFA House Price Index]] | Quarterly + Monthly |
| Mortgage Rates (30yr) | [[FRED Housing Series]] | Weekly |
| Homeownership Rate | [[Census Bureau Housing Data]] | Quarterly |

## Key Metro Indicators

| Indicator | Primary Source | Granularity |
|-----------|---------------|------------|
| Home Values (ZHVI) | [[Zillow Research Data]] | Metro / ZIP |
| Median Sale Price | [[Redfin Data Center]] | Metro |
| Active Inventory | [[Realtor.com Research]] | Metro |
| Days on Market | [[Redfin Data Center]] | Metro |
| Fair Market Rent | [[HUD User Datasets]] | Metro / County |

## Recent Housing Reports

### National
```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date AS "Date",
  report_type AS "Type"
FROM "01_Data_Sources/Housing_Real_Estate/National"
SORT date DESC
LIMIT 10
```

### Metro
```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date AS "Date",
  region AS "Metro",
  report_type AS "Type"
FROM "01_Data_Sources/Housing_Real_Estate/Metro"
SORT date DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Affordability Context

- Fair Market Rents: [[HUD User Datasets]]
- Income Limits: [[HUD User Datasets]]
- Mortgage Application Index: [[Mortgage Bankers Association]]
- Delinquency Rates: [[Mortgage Bankers Association]]

## Getting Started

1. Install the **Dataview** community plugin for tables to render
2. Mark sources as `integrated: true` in their frontmatter as you connect them
3. Use the **Housing Report** template in `03_Templates/` to write new reports
4. Link reports to the sources they reference using `[[wikilinks]]`

> Housing data sources, reports, and key indicators. Requires the **Dataview** plugin.

## Housing Data Sources

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Source",
  pricing AS "Pricing",
  priority AS "Priority",
  provides AS "Key Data",
  integrated AS "Live?"
FROM "01_Data_Sources/Housing_Real_Estate"
SORT priority ASC, name ASC
```

## Key National Indicators

| Indicator | Primary Source | Update Frequency |
|-----------|---------------|-----------------|
| Case-Shiller Home Price Index | [[FRED Housing Series]] | Monthly (2-month lag) |
| Housing Starts & Permits | [[Census Bureau Housing Data]] | Monthly |
| FHFA House Price Index | [[FHFA House Price Index]] | Quarterly + Monthly |
| Mortgage Rates (30yr) | [[FRED Housing Series]] | Weekly |
| Homeownership Rate | [[Census Bureau Housing Data]] | Quarterly |

## Key Metro Indicators

| Indicator | Primary Source | Granularity |
|-----------|---------------|------------|
| Home Values (ZHVI) | [[Zillow Research Data]] | Metro / ZIP |
| Median Sale Price | [[Redfin Data Center]] | Metro |
| Active Inventory | [[Realtor.com Research]] | Metro |
| Days on Market | [[Redfin Data Center]] | Metro |
| Fair Market Rent | [[HUD User Datasets]] | Metro / County |

## Recent Housing Reports

### National
```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date AS "Date",
  report_type AS "Type"
FROM "01_Data_Sources/Housing_Real_Estate/National"
SORT date DESC
LIMIT 10
```

### Metro
```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Report",
  date AS "Date",
  region AS "Metro",
  report_type AS "Type"
FROM "01_Data_Sources/Housing_Real_Estate/Metro"
SORT date DESC
LIMIT 10
```

## Affordability Context

- Fair Market Rents: [[HUD User Datasets]]
- Income Limits: [[HUD User Datasets]]
- Mortgage Application Index: [[Mortgage Bankers Association]]
- Delinquency Rates: [[Mortgage Bankers Association]]

## Getting Started

1. Install the **Dataview** community plugin for tables to render
2. Mark sources as `integrated: true` in their frontmatter as you connect them
3. Use the **Housing Report** template in `03_Templates/` to write new reports
4. Link reports to the sources they reference using `[[wikilinks]]`

title: Data Freshness

tags: [dashboard, freshness, maintenance]
last_updated: 2026-03-27

# Data Freshness

Summary:
- Freshness monitor for generated pull notes across all domains.
- Use this dashboard before rerunning pullers so refresh effort stays targeted.

## Stale Data (7+ Days Old)

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Pull",
  domain AS "Domain",
  source AS "Source",
  date_pulled AS "Last Pull",
  dateformat(date(today) - date(date_pulled), "d") AS "Days Stale"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE date_pulled != null AND (date(today) - date(date_pulled)) > dur(7 days)
SORT date_pulled ASC
```

## Fresh Data (Last 3 Days)

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  file.link AS "Pull",
  domain AS "Domain",
  source AS "Source",
  choice(signal_status = "critical", "🔴",
    choice(signal_status = "alert", "🟠",
      choice(signal_status = "watch", "🟡", "⚪"))) AS "Signal"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE date_pulled != null AND (date(today) - date(date_pulled)) <= dur(3 days)
SORT date_pulled DESC
```

## Pull Counts by Domain

```dataview
TABLE WITHOUT ID
  domain AS "Domain",
  length(rows) AS "Total Pulls"
FROM "05_Data_Pulls"
WHERE domain != null
GROUP BY domain
SORT length(rows) DESC
```

## Recommended Refresh Schedule

| Domain | Frequency | Command |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| Macro (Rates) | Weekly | `node run.mjs fred --group rates` |
| Macro (Labor) | Monthly | `node run.mjs fred --group labor` |
| Macro (Inflation) | Monthly | `node run.mjs fred --group inflation` |
| Macro (GDP) | Quarterly | `node run.mjs bea --gdp` |
| Housing | Monthly | `node run.mjs fred --group housing` |
| Biotech | Monthly | `node run.mjs fda --recent-approvals` |
| Government | Monthly | `node run.mjs openfema --recent` |
| Government | Monthly | `node run.mjs usaspending --recent` |
| Market Quotes | On-demand | `node run.mjs alphavantage --quote SPY` |
| News | Weekly | `node run.mjs newsapi --topic business` |
| Local Data | Monthly | `node run.mjs socrata --permits` |
| Treasury | Monthly | `node run.mjs treasury --yields` |

---
title: InfraNodus Graph Measurements
type: reference
tags: [reference, graph, infranodus]
last_updated: 2026-03-27
---

# InfraNodus Graph Measurements

Summary:
- Measurement workflow for using InfraNodus on top of the Obsidian graph.
- Read this before running graph sessions or interpreting clusters, bridges, and gaps.

This vault treats InfraNodus as the measurement layer on top of the Obsidian graph. Obsidian's graph is useful for shape; InfraNodus is useful for cluster detection, bridge discovery, and gap analysis.

## Vault Defaults

The plugin is tuned for this vault's structure:

- `Single page processing`: `[[Wiki Links]] and Concepts`
- `Multi page processing`: `[[Wiki Links]] Prioritized`
- `Link mentions to`: `parent page and others in the same paragraph`
- `Default graph layer`: `topics`
- `Export graph name`: `my_data_*`
- `Export AI context name`: `my_data_ai_*`

Why:

- Single-note analysis needs concepts because thesis notes carry meaning in the body, not only in links.
- Folder analysis should prioritize explicit wiki links so measurement stays anchored to the vault's graph structure.
- Parent-page links make it easier to trace a concept cluster back to the note that owns it.

## What Each Mode Is For

| Mode | Use it for | Main output |
|------|------------|-------------|
| `topics` | Start here for most folder measurements | Dominant clusters and their anchor terms |
| `concepts` | Single-note idea mapping | Salient terms inside one note |
| `gaps` | Missing bridges | Themes that should connect but do not |
| `trends` | Time-based evidence folders | What is changing in recent pull notes |
| `graph` | Final structural check | Raw network shape and weakly connected islands |

## Best Measurement Scopes

| Scope | What to look for | Typical action |
|-------|------------------|----------------|
| `08_Entities/` | Tight sector-country-commodity clusters | Add bridge notes where adjacent clusters barely touch |
| `09_Macro/` | Regime-indicator structure | Add missing parent regime or indicator links |
| `10_Theses/` | Overlapping theses | Merge duplicates or create a stronger parent thesis |
| `05_Data_Pulls/<domain>/` | Repeating evidence terms | Turn repeated signals into a thesis note or signal note |
| `01_Data_Sources/` | Catalog overlap and blind spots | Add missing pullers or mark source redundancy |

## Measurement Workflow

1. Open one folder or one note, not the whole vault.
2. Start in `topics` mode and note the strongest clusters.
3. Switch to `gaps` and identify missing bridge notes, unresolved links, or absent comparative notes.
4. Switch to `graph` to confirm the network shape is real and not a concept-only artifact.
5. Save the result as a Graph Session note with actions.

## CLI Measurement

You can generate an automated session note from the same InfraNodus backend the plugin uses:

```powershell
node run.mjs infranodus --path 10_Theses
node run.mjs infranodus --path 05_Data_Pulls/Government
```

The command writes a dated note to `07_Playbooks/Graph_Sessions/` with topic clusters, gap candidates, concept noise, and follow-up actions.

## Reading The Results

- A strong cluster means the vault already has a coherent theme there.
- A strong bridge node is usually a thesis note, regime note, or major entity that deserves careful maintenance.
- A gap is not automatically a problem; it becomes actionable when two related themes should already be linked for investment reasoning.
- If `topics` is noisy, the underlying notes are usually too table-heavy or inconsistent in naming.

## Writing Notes For Better Measurement

- Prefer canonical `[[Wiki Links]]` over aliases.
- Keep one claim or observation per paragraph or bullet.
- Add a short `TL;DR` and `Key change` section above large tables in pull notes.
- Use the same entity name every time; do not mix `Google`, `Alphabet`, and `GOOGL` unless one is the canonical note.
- When a pull note introduces a new recurring concept, link it to an entity, macro regime, or thesis note immediately.

## Action Thresholds

- Create a bridge note when the same gap appears in two or more measurement sessions.
- Create or repair an entity note when a concept repeats across pull notes but has no canonical `[[Wiki Link]]`.
- Refactor a thesis when it sits in multiple clusters but has weak explicit links.

## Related Notes

- [[Network Graph]]
- [[Graph Conventions]]
- [[Vault_Schemas]]
